skill.name: clickup-integration-expert
skill.description: Guides synchronization between local roadmaps and ClickUp using the official ClickUp MCP.
skill.summary: Map, sync, and reconcile local roadmap items with ClickUp tasks.
skill.author: AndresNaza

security_audit.summary: The static external-command and weak-cryptography findings are false positives caused by Markdown code fences, inline command names, and table text. The only confirmed concern is a documented setup command that adds the official ClickUp MCP endpoint and requires OAuth, which is expected for this integration.

low_findings.title: Documented ClickUp MCP Network Endpoint
low_findings.description: The skill instructs users to add the official ClickUp MCP endpoint and authenticate with OAuth. This is expected functionality, but users should understand that roadmap data may be sent to ClickUp during sync operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The endpoint and OAuth step are clearly documented, and they match the stated ClickUp integration purpose. I found no evidence of hidden or unrelated network destinations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backtick Execution
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution findings point to Markdown code fences, inline command names, and documentation tables. No executable Ruby code or shell backtick substitution is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown documentation and command examples, not runnable skill code. The package contains only SKILL.md, so there is no script execution path.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Detection
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptographic algorithm findings occur on the frontmatter description and a Markdown table header. No hashing, encryption, or cryptographic API usage is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact cited lines contain descriptive text and table formatting only. There is no code that implements MD5, SHA-1, or another weak cryptographic primitive.

content.value_statement: Teams often lose alignment when local roadmap files and ClickUp tasks drift apart. This skill provides mapping rules and sync guidance for ClickUp MCP workflows.
actual_capabilities: Maps local epics to ClickUp tasks with an Epic type.
Maps local milestones to ClickUp subtasks under epics.
Documents setup for the official ClickUp MCP endpoint.
Defines status mappings between roadmap states and ClickUp states.
Provides basic conflict resolution rules for status, points, and title fields.
limitations: Requires a configured ClickUp MCP connection and OAuth authentication.
Does not include executable sync scripts or API implementation code.
Covers the documented roadmap schema only.
Conflict rules are simple and may need project-specific changes.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Roadmap Sync
use_cases.description: Set up ClickUp workspace, space, and folder mappings before the first sync.

use_cases.title: Review Sync Status
use_cases.description: Check whether local epics and milestones are linked to the correct ClickUp tasks.

use_cases.title: Resolve Roadmap Conflicts
use_cases.description: Apply clear ownership rules when ClickUp and local roadmap fields disagree.

prompt_templates.title: Set Up ClickUp Sync
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new repository.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me configure ClickUp sync for my local roadmap. Walk me through the workspace, space, folder, and OAuth setup.

prompt_templates.title: Map Roadmap Items
prompt_templates.scenario: Create or review mapping between local items and ClickUp tasks.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my roadmap structure and explain how each epic and milestone should map to ClickUp tasks and subtasks.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Dry Run
prompt_templates.scenario: Preview changes before pushing or pulling data.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me plan a dry-run sync. Identify what should be pulled from ClickUp and what should be pushed from the local roadmap.

prompt_templates.title: Resolve Sync Conflicts
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced conflict review after remote and local changes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze these roadmap and ClickUp differences. Apply the documented conflict rules and recommend the final values for status, points, and title.

output_examples.input: I need to sync a local epic named User Auth with ClickUp.
output_examples.output: Create or link a ClickUp task with type Epic for User Auth. Store the task identifier in the roadmap sync mapping.

output_examples.input: ClickUp says a task is complete, but my local roadmap says in progress.
output_examples.output: Use ClickUp as the source of truth for status. Update the local roadmap status to done after confirming the task match.

output_examples.input: I want to push changes but avoid overwriting remote work.
output_examples.output: Pull remote changes first, run a dry-run preview, review status conflicts, then push only the confirmed local planning fields.

best_practices: Pull from ClickUp before pushing local roadmap changes.
Use dry-run mode to preview sync changes before writing data.
Check sync status before linking or unlinking roadmap items.
anti_patterns: Do not push local changes before reviewing recent ClickUp updates.
Do not manually edit sync mappings without verifying task identifiers.
Do not assume conflict rules fit every team without review.

faq.question: Does this skill connect to ClickUp by itself?
faq.answer: No. It documents how to use the official ClickUp MCP and requires separate OAuth authentication.

faq.question: What local items does it map?
faq.answer: It maps epics to ClickUp tasks and milestones to subtasks under those epic tasks.

faq.question: Which status values are supported?
faq.answer: The documented mappings are planned to To Do, in-progress to In Progress, and done to Complete.

faq.question: How are conflicts handled?
faq.answer: ClickUp wins for status, while local roadmap values win for points and title.

faq.question: Can I preview changes before syncing?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill recommends using dry-run previews before applying sync changes.

faq.question: Is this safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found no malicious code, but users should review the ClickUp MCP network connection.
